title: Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) season 9

infobox.key: No. of episodes
Original network
infobox.value: [CBS](/wiki/CBS "CBS")

infobox.key: Original release
No. overall
infobox.value: No. in season | Title | Directed by | Written by | Original release date | Prod. code
1 | "Nine Dragons" | Michael O'Herlihy | Jerome Coopersmith | September 30, 1976 (1976-09-30) | 1310-1729-0601
3 | "Assault on the Palace" | Ernest Pintoff | Jerome Coopersmith | October 7, 1976 (1976-10-07) | 1310-1729-0606

infobox.key: McGarrett must solve a daring bank robbery committed during the [King Kamehameha Day](/wiki/King_Kamehameha_I_Day "King Kamehameha I Day") parade under the cover of a historic re-enactment. [Clu Gulager](/wiki/Clu_Gulager "Clu Gulager") and [Michael McGuire](/wiki/Michael_McGuire_(actor) "Michael McGuire (actor)") guest stars.

infobox.value: 4 | "Oldest Profession -- Latest Price" | Philip Leacock | Anne Collins | October 14, 1976 (1976-10-14) | 1310-1729-0607

infobox.key: Five-O discovers that the murders of prostitutes on the islands appears to be linked to an extortion racketeer. [Elaine Joyce](/wiki/Elaine_Joyce "Elaine Joyce") and [Ned Beatty](/wiki/Ned_Beatty "Ned Beatty") guest stars.

infobox.value: 5 | "Man on Fire" | Gordon Hessler | Stephen Kandel | October 21, 1976 (1976-10-21) | 1310-1729-0604

infobox.key: When Five-O discovers that five murder victims were killed by radiation poisoning, they must track down the one person they all came in contact with before it claims another victim. [Pat Hingle](/wiki/Pat_Hingle "Pat Hingle"), [John Hillerman](/wiki/John_Hillerman "John Hillerman") and [Alan Fudge](/wiki/Alan_Fudge "Alan Fudge") guest stars.

infobox.value: 6 | "Tour de Force, Killer Aboard" | Jerry London | Charles Larson | October 28, 1976 (1976-10-28) | 1310-1729-0608

infobox.key: A lack of information hampers Five-O's search for an international assassin. [Amanda McBroom](/wiki/Amanda_McBroom "Amanda McBroom") and [Cliff Gorman](/wiki/Cliff_Gorman "Cliff Gorman") guest stars.

infobox.value: 7 | "The Last of the Great Paperhangers" | Philip Leacock | Orville H. Hampton | November 4, 1976 (1976-11-04) | 1310-1729-0610

infobox.key: A forger ([Kevin McCarthy](/wiki/Kevin_McCarthy_(actor) "Kevin McCarthy (actor)")) steals over $14,000 from the Five-O team, then targets a bank and the U.S. Navy. [Elaine Giftos](/wiki/Elaine_Giftos "Elaine Giftos") and [Anthony Ponzini](/wiki/Anthony_Ponzini "Anthony Ponzini") guest stars.

infobox.value: 8 | "Heads, You're Dead" | Bruce Bilson | Herman Groves | November 11, 1976 (1976-11-11) | 1310-1729-0609

infobox.key: The annual [Transpacific Yacht Race](/wiki/Transpacific_Yacht_Race "Transpacific Yacht Race") from California to the islands is the scene of mutiny and murder. [Amanda McBroom](/wiki/Amanda_McBroom "Amanda McBroom") guest stars.

infobox.value: 9 | "Let Death Do Us Part" | Barry Crane | Bud Freeman | November 18, 1976 (1976-11-18) | 1310-1729-0614

infobox.key: Rather than accept parole, a prisoner escapes to prove he did not kill his wife. [Zohra Lampert](/wiki/Zohra_Lampert "Zohra Lampert"), [Jack Kelly](/wiki/Jack_Kelly_(actor) "Jack Kelly (actor)") and [Lyle Bettger](/wiki/Lyle_Bettger "Lyle Bettger") guest stars.

infobox.value: 10 | "Double Exposure" | Sutton Roley | Dean Tait & David Deutsch | December 2, 1976 (1976-12-02) | 1310-1729-0617

infobox.key: A mobster who staged his own "death" to elude a conviction returns to reclaim his former syndicate. [Meg Foster](/wiki/Meg_Foster "Meg Foster"), [George Wyner](/wiki/George_Wyner "George Wyner"), [Seth Sakai](/wiki/Seth_Sakai "Seth Sakai") and [Thayer David](/wiki/Thayer_David "Thayer David") guest stars.

infobox.value: 11 | "Yes, My Deadly Daughter" | Bruce Bilson | *S* : James Menzies *T* : Tim Maschler | December 16, 1976 (1976-12-16) | 1310-1729-0613

infobox.key: The daughter of a crime lord is murdering her father's appointed successors. [Irene Yah-Ling Sun](/wiki/Irene_Yah-Ling_Sun "Irene Yah-Ling Sun"), [Paul Hecht](/wiki/Paul_Hecht "Paul Hecht"), [Kwan Hi Lim](/wiki/Kwan_Hi_Lim "Kwan Hi Lim") and [Clyde Kusatsu](/wiki/Clyde_Kusatsu "Clyde Kusatsu") guest stars.

infobox.value: 12 | "Target - A Cop" | Robert Scheerer | Bill Stratton | December 23, 1976 (1976-12-23) | 1310-1729-0612

infobox.key: A vengeful ex-convict ([Don Stroud](/wiki/Don_Stroud "Don Stroud")), partially paralyzed by a police bullet, begins killing police officers. [Gerald McRaney](/wiki/Gerald_McRaney "Gerald McRaney") and [Seth Sakai](/wiki/Seth_Sakai "Seth Sakai") guest stars.

infobox.value: 13 | "The Bells Toll at Noon" | [Jack Lord](/wiki/Jack_Lord "Jack Lord") | *S* : Irv Pearlberg *T* : Charles Larson | January 6, 1977 (1977-01-06) | 1310-1729-0611

infobox.key: An avenging man ([Rich Little](/wiki/Rich_Little "Rich Little")) pursues a drug dealers who contributed to a former addict's death. [Milton Selzer](/wiki/Milton_Selzer "Milton Selzer"), [Don Knight](/wiki/Don_Knight_(actor) "Don Knight (actor)") and [Mel Ferrer](/wiki/Mel_Ferrer "Mel Ferrer") guest stars.

infobox.value: 14 | "Man in a Steel Frame" | Allen Reisner | Robert Stambler | January 13, 1977 (1977-01-13) | 1310-1729-0619

infobox.key: McGarrett is a victim of an elaborate plot to frame him for murder of his girlfriend ([Camilla Sparv](/wiki/Camilla_Sparv "Camilla Sparv")). [Jonathan Goldsmith](/wiki/Jonathan_Goldsmith "Jonathan Goldsmith") and [Danny Kamekona](/wiki/Danny_Kamekona "Danny Kamekona") guest stars.

infobox.value: 15 | "Ready... Aim..." | Jerry London | Tim Maschler | January 20, 1977 (1977-01-20) | 1310-1729-0623

infobox.key: The Five-O team helps a Tokyo cop smash a ring that smuggles handguns into Japan. [France Nuyen](/wiki/France_Nuyen "France Nuyen"), [Manu Tupou](/wiki/Manu_Tupou "Manu Tupou"), [Edward James Olmos](/wiki/Edward_James_Olmos "Edward James Olmos") and [Roger Perry](/wiki/Roger_Perry "Roger Perry") guest stars.

infobox.value: 16 | "Elegy in a Rain Forest" | Sutton Roley | Herman Groves | January 27, 1977 (1977-01-27) | 1310-1729-0620
17 | "Dealer's Choice...Blackmail" | Ernest Pintoff | Tim Maschler | February 3, 1977 (1977-02-03) | 1310-1729-0605
18 | "A Capitol Crime" | Sutton Roley | *S* : James Menzies *T* : Bill Stratton | February 17, 1977 (1977-02-17) | 1310-1729-0624
19 | "To Die in Paradise" | Joe Manduke | Bill Stratton | February 24, 1977 (1977-02-24) | 1310-1729-0603

infobox.key: A singer ([Pamela Franklin](/wiki/Pamela_Franklin "Pamela Franklin")) held for ransom is shipwrecked with her kidnappers ([Christopher Connelly](/wiki/Christopher_Connelly "Christopher Connelly") and Stephen Young

infobox.value: 20 | "Blood Money is Hard to Wash" | Allen Reisner | *S* : Curtis Kenyon *T* : Bill Stratton | March 3, 1977 (1977-03-03) | 1310-1729-0622
21 | "To Kill a Mind" | Gordon Hessler | Stephen Kandel | March 17, 1977 (1977-03-17) | 1310-1729-0616

infobox.key: The kidnapping of an oceanographer's brother is linked to the recovery of a computer. [Pat Hingle](/wiki/Pat_Hingle "Pat Hingle") and [Mel Ferrer](/wiki/Mel_Ferrer "Mel Ferrer") guest stars.

infobox.value: 22 | "Requiem for a Saddle Bronc Rider" | Harry Harris | Herman Groves | March 24, 1977 (1977-03-24) | 1310-1729-0615

infobox.key: The Five-O team are finding a lot of resistance while searching for a cowboy at the rodeo grounds where he was working. [George DiCenzo](/wiki/George_DiCenzo "George DiCenzo") and [Nicholas Hammond](/wiki/Nicholas_Hammond "Nicholas Hammond") guest stars

infobox.value: 23 | "See How She Runs" | Harvey Laidman | Anne Collins | March 31, 1977 (1977-03-31) | 1310-1729-0621

infobox.key: The team track down the daughter ([Jessica Harper](/wiki/Jessica_Harper "Jessica Harper")) of a Los Angeles lawman ([Biff McGuire](/wiki/Biff_McGuire "Biff McGuire")) who has taken refuge with a religious cult after she is framed for murder. However, the real person ([Paul Shenar](/wiki/Paul_Shenar "Paul Shenar")) that was responsible for the crime is also searching for her.

infobox.value: 24 | "Practical Jokes Can Kill You" | Ernest Pintoff | Bill Stratton | May 5, 1977 (1977-05-05) | 1310-1729-0618

infobox.key: A hang glider's theft of a historical exhibit catches the attention of crooks planning a heist of Army rifles. [Lee Purcell](/wiki/Lee_Purcell "Lee Purcell"), [Charles Frank](/wiki/Charles_Frank "Charles Frank"), [Allan Rich](/wiki/Allan_Rich "Allan Rich") guest stars.

This is a list of episodes from the ninth season of *[Hawaii Five-O](/wiki/Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series) "Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series)")*. [Herman Wedemeyer](/wiki/Herman_Wedemeyer "Herman Wedemeyer") is elevated to main cast member.

## Broadcast history

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_9&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Broadcast history")]

The season originally aired Thursdays at 9:00-10:00 pm (EST).[[1]](#cite_note-1)[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## DVD release

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_9&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: DVD release")]

The season was released on DVD by Paramount Home Video.

## Episodes

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_season_9&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Episodes")]

See also: [List of Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) episodes](/wiki/List_of_Hawaii_Five-O_(1968_TV_series)_episodes "List of Hawaii Five-O (1968 TV series) episodes")

| The probe of a theft of nerve gas puts McGarrett on Wo Fat's ([Khigh Dhiegh](/wiki/Khigh_Dhiegh "Khigh Dhiegh")) trail, which leads to Hong Kong and a case of amnesia for himself. [Dina Merrill](/wiki/Dina_Merrill "Dina Merrill"), [David Tomlinson](/wiki/David_Tomlinson "David Tomlinson"), [Michael Anderson Jr.](/wiki/Michael_Anderson_Jr. "Michael Anderson Jr.") and [Ric Young](/wiki/Ric_Young "Ric Young") guest stars. **NOTE:** Shown in syndication in two parts. | | | | | | | | | | | |

| McGarrett is a victim of an elaborate plot to frame him for murder of his girlfriend ([Camilla Sparv](/wiki/Camilla_Sparv "Camilla Sparv")). [Jonathan Goldsmith](/wiki/Jonathan_Goldsmith "Jonathan Goldsmith") and [Danny Kamekona](/wiki/Danny_Kamekona "Danny Kamekona") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 208 | 15 | "Ready... Aim..." | Jerry London | Tim Maschler | January 20, 1977 (1977-01-20) | 1310-1729-0623 |
| The Five-O team helps a Tokyo cop smash a ring that smuggles handguns into Japan. [France Nuyen](/wiki/France_Nuyen "France Nuyen"), [Manu Tupou](/wiki/Manu_Tupou "Manu Tupou"), [Edward James Olmos](/wiki/Edward_James_Olmos "Edward James Olmos") and [Roger Perry](/wiki/Roger_Perry "Roger Perry") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 209 | 16 | "Elegy in a Rain Forest" | Sutton Roley | Herman Groves | January 27, 1977 (1977-01-27) | 1310-1729-0620 |
| McGarrett fears a rapist-murderer ([William C. Watson](/wiki/William_C._Watson "William C. Watson")) roaming Oahu will find a girl (Laurie Prange) lost in the mountains before Five-O can rescue her. **NOTE:** Final series appearance of [Glenn Cannon](/wiki/Glenn_Cannon "Glenn Cannon") in the role of District Attorney John Manicote. | | | | | | |
| 210 | 17 | "Dealer's Choice...Blackmail" | Ernest Pintoff | Tim Maschler | February 3, 1977 (1977-02-03) | 1310-1729-0605 |
| The head of a gambling syndicate kills a police officer in a hit-and-run auto accident. [John Ritter](/wiki/John_Ritter "John Ritter"), [Nehemiah Persoff](/wiki/Nehemiah_Persoff "Nehemiah Persoff") guest stars. **NOTE:** Final series appearance of [Amanda McBroom](/wiki/Amanda_McBroom "Amanda McBroom") in the role of HPD officer Sandi Welles. | | | | | | |
| 211 | 18 | "A Capitol Crime" | Sutton Roley | *S* : James Menzies *T* : Bill Stratton | February 17, 1977 (1977-02-17) | 1310-1729-0624 |
| An elderly man ([Barnard Hughes](/wiki/Barnard_Hughes "Barnard Hughes")) wires himself to a bomb and takes hostages to halt his home's demolition. [Sharon Farrell](/wiki/Sharon_Farrell "Sharon Farrell") and [Richard Davalos](/wiki/Richard_Davalos "Richard Davalos") guest stars. **NOTE:** Jimmy Borges in the role as himself. | | | | | | |
| 212 | 19 | "To Die in Paradise" | Joe Manduke | Bill Stratton | February 24, 1977 (1977-02-24) | 1310-1729-0603 |
| A singer ([Pamela Franklin](/wiki/Pamela_Franklin "Pamela Franklin")) held for ransom is shipwrecked with her kidnappers ([Christopher Connelly](/wiki/Christopher_Connelly "Christopher Connelly") and Stephen Young | | | | | | |
| 213 | 20 | "Blood Money is Hard to Wash" | Allen Reisner | *S* : Curtis Kenyon *T* : Bill Stratton | March 3, 1977 (1977-03-03) | 1310-1729-0622 |
| Mainland gangster needing a place to launder money tries to force a football-club owner to sell his team. [Dane Clark](/wiki/Dane_Clark "Dane Clark") and [Jo Anne Worley](/wiki/Jo_Anne_Worley "Jo Anne Worley") guest stars. **NOTE:** Lynne Kimoto as McGarrett's secretary, Lani. | | | | | | |
| 214 | 21 | "To Kill a Mind" | Gordon Hessler | Stephen Kandel | March 17, 1977 (1977-03-17) | 1310-1729-0616 |
| The kidnapping of an oceanographer's brother is linked to the recovery of a computer. [Pat Hingle](/wiki/Pat_Hingle "Pat Hingle") and [Mel Ferrer](/wiki/Mel_Ferrer "Mel Ferrer") guest stars. | | | | | | |
| 215 | 22 | "Requiem for a Saddle Bronc Rider" | Harry Harris | Herman Groves | March 24, 1977 (1977-03-24) | 1310-1729-0615 |
| The Five-O team are finding a lot of resistance while searching for a cowboy at the rodeo grounds where he was working. [George DiCenzo](/wiki/George_DiCenzo "George DiCenzo") and [Nicholas Hammond](/wiki/Nicholas_Hammond "Nicholas Hammond") guest stars | | | | | | |
| 216 | 23 | "See How She Runs" | Harvey Laidman | Anne Collins | March 31, 1977 (1977-03-31) | 1310-1729-0621 |
| The team track down the daughter ([Jessica Harper](/wiki/Jessica_Harper "Jessica Harper")) of a Los Angeles lawman ([Biff McGuire](/wiki/Biff_McGuire "Biff McGuire")) who has taken refuge with a religious cult after she is framed for murder. However, the real person ([Paul Shenar](/wiki/Paul_Shenar "Paul Shenar")) that was responsible for the crime is also searching for her. | | | | | | |
| 217 | 24 | "Practical Jokes Can Kill You" | Ernest Pintoff | Bill Stratton | May 5, 1977 (1977-05-05) | 1310-1729-0618 |
| A hang glider's theft of a historical exhibit catches the attention of crooks planning a heist of Army rifles. [Lee Purcell](/wiki/Lee_Purcell "Lee Purcell"), [Charles Frank](/wiki/Charles_Frank "Charles Frank"), [Allan Rich](/wiki/Allan_Rich "Allan Rich") guest stars. | | | | | | |
